2026-04-13T09:12:41.9275001Z {"add_comment": {"item_number": 58111, "body": "## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage\n\n**Summary:** Blazor WebAssembly applications encounter CORS preflight failures when using `Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter` because the `x-ms-return-client-request-id` request header is blocked by the Application Insights ingestion endpoint's `Access-Control-Allow-Headers` policy. The author requests native C# Blazor WASM support without requiring JavaScript bridge workarounds.\n\n<details>\n<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>\n\n**Package:** `Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter` (version not specified)\n**Affected API:** `ApplicationInsightsRestClient` — HTTP request headers sent to `https://*.in.applicationinsights.azure.com/v2.1/track`\n**Scenarios:**\n- Blazor WASM app using `Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter` fails to export telemetry due to CORS preflight rejection\n- The blocked header is `x-ms-return-client-request-id` which is not listed in `Access-Control-Allow-Headers` in the preflight response\n- JavaScript SDK and third-party workaround `BlazorApplicationInsights` both work, but a native C# solution is desired\n\n**Root ask:** Native Blazor WASM support for `Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter` — either by removing/conditionally omitting the `x-ms-return-client-request-id` header in browser environments, or by the ingestion endpoint allowing the header in CORS preflight responses. References upstream OpenTelemetry .NET PR [#6379](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-dotnet/pull/6379) for `BatchExportProcessor` WASM support.\n</details>\n\n<details>\n<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>\n\nThe CORS error indicates that the `x-ms-return-client-request-id` header (likely added by `Azure.Core`'s `RequestClientRequestIdPolicy`) is not in the `Access-Control-Allow-Headers` of the CORS preflight response from the Application Insights ingestion endpoint. Browser fetch API enforces this restriction strictly.\n\n**Suggested investigation steps:**\n1. Confirm whether `x-ms-return-client-request-id` is added by `Azure.Core` pipeline policies and whether it can be suppressed or configured off for browser/WASM environments.\n2. Check if the Application Insights ingestion endpoint (`*.in.applicationinsights.azure.com`) can be updated to include `x-ms-return-client-request-id` in its CORS `Access-Control-Allow-Headers` — this may require coordination with the backend service team.\n3. Review the upstream OpenTelemetry .NET PR #6379 for `BatchExportProcessor` single-threaded WASM support and assess readiness for adoption in `Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter`.\n4. Assess whether a Blazor WASM-specific configuration or target platform check (`net8.0-browser` / `browser-wasm`) could be added to conditionally disable or replace headers that are incompatible with browser CORS policies.\n</details>\n\n<details>\n<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>\n\n- **Category:** `Client` — This is a client SDK behavior issue (a request header injected by the SDK causes CORS failures); it is not a management-plane or provisioning issue.\n- **Service:** `Monitor - Exporter` — The issue explicitly names `Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter` which lives under `/sdk/monitor/Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter/`.\n- **Confidence:** High — Package is explicitly named in the issue body. Three quality reference issues (#38991, #37991, #24782) all use `Client` + `Monitor - Exporter` for this same package. No ambiguity between services or categories.\n</details>\n\n<details>\n<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>\n\n- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %Monitor - Exporter` (line 363) — matches because the predicted service label `Monitor - Exporter` aligns exactly with this entry's single label requirement.\n- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed\n- **ServiceOwners:** @harsimar @mattsains-msft @rajkumar-rangaraj @xiang17\n- **Routing action:** Added `Service Attention` and `needs-team-attention` labels; posted `mention_owners` comment to @-mention all ServiceOwners; issue left unassigned (no AzureSdkOwners).\n- **Scan notes:** Bottom-to-top scan from line 1228 encountered entries for WebPubSub, Voice Live, TimeseriesInsights, Tables, Synapse, Storage, SignalR, Service Bus, Search, Schema Registry, Provisioning, PostgreSQL Auth, Planetary Computer, OpenAI, Online Experimentation, Network DNS, Network, Monitor - Query — none matched `%Monitor - Exporter`. First match was `%Monitor - Exporter` at line 363.\n</details>"}}

2026-04-13T09:13:16.4066112Z {"add_comment": {"item_number": 58111, "body": "## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage\n\n**Summary:** Blazor WebAssembly applications encounter CORS preflight failures when using `Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter` because the `x-ms-return-client-request-id` request header is blocked by the Application Insights ingestion endpoint's `Access-Control-Allow-Headers` policy. The author requests native C# Blazor WASM support without JavaScript bridge workarounds.\n\n<details>\n<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>\n\n**Package:** `Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter` (version not specified)\n**Affected API:** `ApplicationInsightsRestClient` — HTTP requests to `https://*.in.applicationinsights.azure.com/v2.1/track`\n**Scenarios:**\n- Blazor WASM app fails to export telemetry due to CORS preflight rejection of the `x-ms-return-client-request-id` header\n- JavaScript SDK works; third-party `BlazorApplicationInsights` workaround works — but native C# solution is absent\n- Author references upstream OpenTelemetry .NET PR #6379 for single-threaded WASM `BatchExportProcessor` support\n\n**Root ask:** Native Blazor WASM support in `Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter` — either by conditionally omitting browser-incompatible headers or by updating the ingestion endpoint CORS policy.\n</details>\n\n<details>\n<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>\n\nThe `x-ms-return-client-request-id` header is likely injected by `Azure.Core`'s `RequestClientRequestIdPolicy`. Browser CORS strictly enforces the `Access-Control-Allow-Headers` preflight contract; if the ingestion endpoint does not list this header, the browser will block the request.\n\n**Suggested investigation steps:**\n1. Confirm whether `x-ms-return-client-request-id` is added by `Azure.Core` pipeline policies and whether it can be suppressed or conditionally omitted for browser/WASM runtimes.\n2. Check if the Application Insights ingestion endpoint can be updated to include `x-ms-return-client-request-id` in CORS `Access-Control-Allow-Headers` — requires service team coordination.\n3. Review upstream OpenTelemetry .NET PR #6379 for `BatchExportProcessor` WASM readiness and plan adoption in this package.\n4. Evaluate whether a `net8.0-browser` / WASM-specific build target or runtime check can disable headers incompatible with browser CORS policies.\n</details>\n\n<details>\n<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>\n\n- **Category:** `Client` — SDK client library behavior issue (header injected by SDK triggers CORS failure); not management-plane or provisioning.\n- **Service:** `Monitor - Exporter` — Package `Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter` is explicitly named in the issue body; lives at `/sdk/monitor/Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter/`.\n- **Confidence:** High — Package explicitly named; 3 quality reference issues (#38991, #37991, #24782) all use `Client` + `Monitor - Exporter` for this same package. No ambiguity.\n</details>\n\n<details>\n<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>\n\n- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %Monitor - Exporter` (line 363) — matched because predicted service label `Monitor - Exporter` satisfies this entry's single-label requirement.\n- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed\n- **ServiceOwners:** @harsimar @mattsains-msft @rajkumar-rangaraj @xiang17\n- **Routing action:** Added `Service Attention` + `needs-team-attention` labels; `mention_owners` posted to notify all ServiceOwners; issue left unassigned (no AzureSdkOwners defined).\n- **Scan notes:** Bottom-to-top scan from line 1228 passed through WebPubSub, Voice Live, TimeseriesInsights, Tables, Synapse, Storage, SignalR, Service Bus, Search, Schema Registry, Provisioning, PostgreSQL Auth, Planetary Computer, OpenAI, Online Experimentation, Network DNS, Network, Monitor-Query — none matched `%Monitor - Exporter`. First match was `%Monitor - Exporter` at line 363.\n</details>"}}

2026-04-13T09:13:20.2067924Z   > **Library name:** Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter
2026-04-13T09:13:20.2068223Z   >
2026-04-13T09:13:20.2068791Z   > **Problem Description:** Blazor WASM Applications having CORS exceptions when using Application Insights with `Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter`.
2026-04-13T09:13:20.2069395Z   >
2026-04-13T09:13:20.2070536Z   > `Access to fetch at 'https://westeurope-5.in.applicationinsights.azure.com/v2.1/track' from origin 'https://localhost:7250' has been blocked by CORS policy: Request header field x-ms-return-client-request-id is not allowed by Access-Control-Allow-Headers in preflight response`
2026-04-13T09:13:20.2071546Z   >
2026-04-13T09:13:20.2073042Z   > The JavaScript SDK works, and there is a Blazor workaround ([BlazorApplicationInsights](https://www.nuget.org/packages/BlazorApplicationInsights)), but the request is for native C# support without workarounds. References the upstream OpenTelemetry PR: [feat: Add Task support for BatchExportProcessor and PeriodicExportingMetricReader for single-threaded WebAssembly](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-dotnet/pull/6379).
